title: Diet for Obesity Management
content: For obesity management, patients should aim to consume less than 200 milligrams (mg) of cholesterol a day. Dietary choices should limit high-fat foods, sugar, and alcohol.

title: Diet for Diabetes Management
content: For diabetes management, patients should strictly avoid high-fat dairy products (like whole milk) and high sugary foods.

title: Diet for Gout Management
content: For gout management, patients should avoid red meats such as beef, seafood, and certain animal proteins (like chicken or fish in excess). These foods contribute to high blood levels of uric acid which can trigger gout attacks.

title: Diet for Multiple Diseases
content: Patients who have multiple concurrent diseases should completely avoid alcohol and should highly consider transitioning to a vegetarian diet rich in vegetables.
